mapping.a_element: quantized worldvolume U(1) fluxes stabilizing D-branes on group manifolds
mapping.b_element: affine extensions of Lie algebras inducing NIM-reps and branching rules

mapping.a_element: Born-Infeld action governing D-brane dynamics and spectrum
mapping.b_element: Verlinde-like formula for branching coefficients derived from affine extensions

mapping.a_element: SU(2) group manifold as the background for D-branes
mapping.b_element: semi-simple Lie algebras (e.g., g = SU(2)) and their affine extensions

mapping.a_element: conformal field theory predictions for D-brane spectrum
mapping.b_element: fusion rings of WZW models with chiral algebra g_k
branching rules and NIM-reps induced by embeddings p in g

brief_justification: While both papers involve group manifolds (e.g., SU(2)) and connect to conformal field theory/WZW models, Paper A focuses on dynamical stabilization of D-branes via quantized fluxes in the Born-Infeld framework, whereas Paper B derives combinatorial branching rules for Lie algebras using affine extensions and NIM-reps. The underlying machinery—flux quantization in string theory vs. algebraic branching rules—is distinct, despite shared group-theoretic and CFT contexts.
